Introductory Programming in R Bao Maddux 2024-04-12 Chapter 1 About R and RStudio 1.1 What is R and RStudio? R is a language and environment for statistical computing and graphics. It is a GNU project which is similar to the S language and environment which was developed at Bell Laboratories (formerly AT&T, now Lucent Technologies) by John Chambers and colleagues. R can be considered as a different implementation of S. There are some important differences, but much code written for S runs unaltered under R. R provides a wide variety of statistical (linear and nonlinear modelling, classical statistical tests, time-series analysis, classification, clustering, …) and graphical techniques, and is highly extensible. The S language is often the vehicle of choice for research in statistical methodology, and R provides an Open Source route to participation in that activity. RStudio is an integrated development environment for R, a programming language for statistical computing and graphics. RStudio offers a copious number of other nice features and a clean editor with syntax highlighting. 1.2 How to Install R and RStudio?
